title: Garmin’s latest patent targets non-invasive glucose estimation
summary: The wearable industry is pushing toward deeper health metrics.
body: TL;DR Garmin has filed a patent describing a method to estimate HbA1c using optical sensors built into its watches. The approach would focus on long-term glucose trends rather than real-time readings. It’s theoretical for now, but echoes the market push for non-invasive metabolic tracking. Glucose tracking is shaping up to be the next major frontier in wearable health. Nearly every major player has been rumored to be chasing a wrist-based solution, and now Garmin appears to be exploring its own approach. A newly surfaced patent, spotted by Wareable , outlines a method for estimating HbA1c, the long-term marker doctors use to evaluate average blood sugar levels. The design uses optical sensors similar to those already built into Garmin watches. Unlike continuous glucose monitors (CGMs), which deliver real-time readings, this approach would focus on longer-term trends. In theory, that could give users a broader view of their metabolic health without finger pricks or external sensors.

title: WHO validates elimination of trachoma as a public health problem in Libya
summary: WHO today announced that Libya has eliminated trachoma as a public health problem, a landmark victory for public health in WHO’s Eastern Mediterranean Region. This hard-won achievement protects future generations from preventable blindness and provides a powerful reminder that countries can overcom…

title: Global commitment on display as countries negotiate key annex to the Pandemic Agreement
summary: Member States of the World Health Organization (WHO) concluded a weeklong round of negotiations on draft annex for Pathogen Access and Benefit Sharing (PABS) – a key component of the WHO Pandemic Agreement. title: Statement on the planned hepatitis B birth dose vaccine trial in Guinea-Bissau
summary: WHO statement on a planned hepatitis B birth dose vaccine trial in Guinea-Bissau.

title: One in two people facing cataract blindness need access to life-changing surgery
summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) is urging countries to accelerate efforts to ensure that millions of people living with cataract can access simple, sight‑restoring surgery – one of the most effective and affordable interventions to prevent avoidable blindness.

title: Four in ten cancer cases could be prevented globally
summary: Up to four in ten cancer cases worldwide could be prevented, according to a new global analysis from the World Health Organization (WHO) and its International Agency for Research on Cancer (IARC). The study examines 30 preventable causes, including tobacco, alcohol, high body mass index, physical i…

title: WHO launches 2026 appeal to help millions of people in health emergencies and crisis settings
summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) today launched its 2026 global appeal to ensure millions of people living in humanitarian crises and conflicts can access health care.

title: Communities unite to address stigma and discrimination affecting people with neglected tropical diseases
summary: Marking World Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) Day, the World Health Organization (WHO) warns that millions of people living with NTDs continue to face profound and often unseen suffering due to discrimination, social stigma and untreated mental health conditions. title: WHO urges schools worldwide to promote healthy eating for children
summary: Healthy food in schools can help children develop healthy dietary habits for life, according to the World Health Organization (WHO), which released a new global guideline on evidence-based policies and interventions to create healthy school food environments.

title: Countries progress negotiations in support of WHO Pandemic Agreement
summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) Member States this week advanced their negotiations on the Pathogen Access and Benefit‑Sharing (PABS) system in a resumed session of the Intergovernmental Working Group (IGWG) on the WHO Pandemic Agreement. The PABS system is a core element of the agreement adopt…

title: WHO renews commitment to a leprosy-free world, spotlighting partnership and progress ahead of World Leprosy Day
summary: Access to treatment for leprosy is essential to global efforts to eliminate leprosy, says the World Health Organization (WHO) ahead of World Leprosy Day, to be observed on 25 January. Leprosy is an infectious disease caused by a type of bacteria, Mycobacterium leprae, and is one of the oldest disea…

title: WHO validates Brazil for eliminating mother-to-child transmission of HIV
summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) has validated Brazil for the elimination of mother-to-child transmission (EMTCT) of HIV, making it the most populous country in the Americas to achieve this historic milestone. This accomplishment reflects Brazil’s long-standing commitment to universal and free a…

title: Countries to reconvene sooner to accelerate progress on WHO Pathogen Access and Benefit Sharing system negotiations
summary: WHO Member States today ended their latest round of intensive negotiations on the world’s first Pathogen Access andBenefit Sharing (PABS) system. Countries decided to resume deliberations in January in a reflection of the shared commitment and urgency needed to help make the world safer from future…

title: New tools saved a million lives from malaria last year but progress under threat as drug resistance rises
summary: This year’s report provides a critical and up-to-date snapshot of efforts to control and eliminate malaria across 80 countries. The report also presents the threat posed by antimalarial resistance and its impact.
